title: Malai Broccoli & Dahi ke Sholay
tagline: Oven-roasted creamy hung-curd broccoli plus crispy baked dahi-paneer bread rolls, off one master marinade.
servingsNote: Snack dinner — broccoli as a side, sholay as the bites
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients across both items.
Protein comes from the hung curd, paneer and cheese (~24 g per serving). Bump it by using paneer made from double-toned milk, or swap 30 g of the paneer for extra hung curd.
The bread in the sholay is the main carb/calorie load — use 4 slices instead of 6, or brown-bread slices, to trim ~120 kcal per serving.
Soya doesn't belong in these delicate items; the paneer + curd already carry the protein.

items: Hung curd (thick, well-drained): 3 tbsp for broccoli + 1/2 cup (120 g) for sholay
6 slices bread, crusts trimmed, flattened with a rolling pin
2 tbsp milk OR a thin slurry (1 tbsp flour + water) for sealing/coating
Breadcrumbs (best) or crushed cornflakes/rusk for the crust

steps.title: Make the master marinade
steps.body: Whisk together the hung curd (keep the two portions separate — 3 tbsp for broccoli, 1/2 cup for sholay), 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 chopped green chilli, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp chaat masala, 1/2 tsp crushed kasuri methi and salt. This is your shared base.

steps.title: Blanch the broccoli
steps.body: Wash ~150 g broccoli florets well and blanch in boiling water for just 60-90 seconds, then drain very well. This step matters — raw broccoli roasts unevenly, and a quick blanch fixes the texture without turning it to mush.

steps.title: Marinate the broccoli
steps.body: To the 3 tbsp marinade portion add 1 tbsp cream/malai, 1 tsp cornflour, 1 tbsp grated cheese and 1/2 tsp lemon juice. Mix to a thick paste, coat the drained broccoli, and rest 20 minutes.

steps.title: Mix the sholay filling
steps.body: To the 1/2 cup marinade portion add 75-100 g crumbled paneer, 1/4 tsp roasted cumin powder, 1 tbsp chopped coriander, 1 tbsp cornflour and 2 tbsp grated cheese. It must be THICK, not runny — if loose, add more cornflour or paneer, or it'll leak while baking.

steps.title: Assemble the sholay
steps.body: Flatten 6 trimmed bread slices with a rolling pin. Put 1-1.5 tbsp filling on each, roll or fold, and seal the edges with a dab of water. Dip each roll in 2 tbsp milk or the thin slurry, then coat in breadcrumbs. Mist or brush with oil.

steps.title: Bake the sholay first
steps.body: Bake the sholay at 200°C for 18-22 minutes, flipping at 10-12 minutes. If still pale at the end, broil/grill 2 minutes for colour.

steps.title: Add the broccoli
steps.body: Spread the marinated broccoli on a lined tray with space between pieces and roast at 220°C for 12-15 minutes (slot it in for the last stretch of the sholay bake). Broil the last 2-3 minutes if you want char.

steps.title: Serve together
steps.body: Both finish around the same time. Serve the malai broccoli hot alongside the crispy dahi ke sholay, with green chutney if you have it.

notes: One master marinade, then split and customise: broccoli wants a clingy creamy coat (cream + cheese + cornflour + lemon); the sholay filling wants to stay tight and low-moisture (paneer + less lemon + extra cornflour) so it doesn't leak.
Oven dahi ke sholay won't be quite as crisp as deep-fried, but with a breadcrumb coat and an oil mist they get surprisingly close.
Efficient timeline: hang the curd, prep + blanch broccoli, mix both marinades, assemble sholay, bake sholay first, add broccoli for the final 12-15 minutes.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
